input.background: A prior illustrative example in this chapter had us conduct a two-sided test of $H_0: p=$ 0.21 using a sample of $n=57$.
input.question: How large a sample is needed to detect a proportion of 0.31 in the population (i.e., $p_1=0.31$ ) at $\alpha=0.05$ (two sided) with $90 \%$ power?

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: \begin{aligned} &n=\left(\frac{z_{1-\alpha / 2} \sqrt{p_0 q_0}+z_{1-\beta }\sqrt{p_1 q_1}}{p_1-p_0}\right)^2=\left(\frac{1.96 \sqrt{(0.21)(0.79)}+1.28 \sqrt{(0.31)(0.69)}}{0.31-0.21}=\right)^2=193.3 .\ &\text { The sample should include } 194 \text { observations. } \end{aligned}

p0.description: The null hypothesis proportion in the population.

p1.description: The alternative hypothesis proportion in the population that we want to detect.

alpha.description: The significance level for the two-sided test.

power.description: The desired power of the test to detect the effect.

n.description: The sample size used in the prior example.
